Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Defendant Unums Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Seeking Production Of Plaintiffs Personnel And Financial Records And For Protective Order
Off Calendar Aug-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Rudy Tap of Pillsbury & Coleman LLP (415-433-800) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party, Thomas J. Purcell

Attorney, Attorney, Robert E. Hess of Hinshaw & Culbertson (949-757-2800) appears telephonically as counsel for defendant/opposing party, Unum Life Insurance Co. of America

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF THOMAS PURCELL'S Motion To Quash Defendant Unums Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Seeking Production Of Plaintiffs Personnel And Financial Records And For Protective Order is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Plaintiff claims total disability from a 2012 injury and that defendant should not have terminated his disability benefits. He moves to quash business record subpoenas to four entities that possess documents regarding him, largely on privacy grounds. I understand that plaintiff has abjured any emotional distress claim (Rply. 7:10) and that defendant has limited the subponeas' time frames to begin in April 2011, removing issues under Pitchess v. Superior Court (1974) 11 Cal.3d 531. Plaintiff has put his medical condition and disability status directly at issue, thus waiving privacy rights. (See Vinson v. Superior Court (1987) 43 Cal.3d 833, 842.) However, the four subponeas are overbroad, reaching beyond relevant subjects. I therefore limit the subpoenas' scope to documents relating to plaintiff's disability status and/or medical condition. I do not limit the parameters of "medical condition," because the record indicates plaintiff may have conditions not caused by the 2012 accident, which may offer alternative or concurrent explanations for his maladies. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR 176, email: geales@sftc.org. (D302)

Rudy Tap, of Pillsbury & Coleman LLP, Tel. 415.433.8000, for plaintiffs;
Robert E. Hess, of Hinshaw & Culbertson LLP, Tel. 949.757.2800, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. Neither party stipulates to the matter being heard by the Judge Pro Tem. PLAINTIFF THOMAS PURCELL Motion To Quash Defendant Unum's Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Seeking Production Of Plaintiffs Personnel And Financial Records And For Protective Order is continued to August 18, 2016, at 9:30 a.m., in Department 302, Law & Motion. The parties are informed that a $30. Court Reporter fee will be assessed and must be paid before the hearing date. Judge Pro Tem to prepare report and recommendation. Judge Pro Tem: Diana Kruze; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.
